papers.title: Uneven Evolution of Cognition Across Generations of Generative AI Models
papers.reason: Excluded because it evaluates the cognitive abilities of generative AI models rather than human participants.

papers.title: ASR-Agnostic Multimodal Spectrotemporal Modeling for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: It develops a speech-based digital biomarker for dementia but contains no interactive decision task, eye tracking, qualifying EEG analysis, or cognitive process model.

papers.title: Demystifying Video Reasoning
papers.reason: Excluded because it studies reasoning and working memory inside diffusion-based video generation models.

papers.title: The Richest Paradigm You're Not Using: Commercial Videogames at the Intersection of Human-Computer Interaction and Cognitive Science
papers.reason: The perspective proposes games, eye tracking, and behavioral timing for executive-function research but does not report an empirical study that measures and analyzes these elements.

papers.title: Pupillometry and Brain Dynamics for Cognitive Load in Working Memory
papers.reason: It analyzes sequential digit-span performance and pupillometry as cognitive-load biomarkers, while the EEG description does not establish frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Quant VideoGen: Auto-Regressive Long Video Generation via 2-Bit KV-Cache Quantization
papers.reason: Excluded because it concerns video generation and KV-cache memory in machine-learning models rather than human cognition.

papers.title: Cognitive Load Estimation Using Brain Foundation Models and Interpretability for BCIs
papers.reason: It addresses continuous human cognitive-load monitoring with EEG, but reports classifier features and regional interpretability without a qualifying task description or frequency-domain/connectivity method.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Computational Model of Brain-Heart Interactions via HRV and EEG Feature
papers.reason: It uses human working-memory task data, but EEG is represented by band-power and generic features and the XGBoost and synthetic-HRV pipeline is not a cognitive generative model.

papers.title: Unveiling the Heart-Brain Connection: An Analysis of ECG in Cognitive Performance
papers.reason: It includes an active working-memory paradigm, but relies on EEG spectral feature summaries and cross-modal XGBoost rather than connectivity analysis or cognitive process modelling.

papers.title: Your Eyes Controlled the Game: Real-Time Cognitive Training Adaptation based on Eye-Tracking and Physiological Data in Virtual Reality
papers.reason: It centrally combines interactive sustained-attention and dual-task VR training with eye tracking to adapt difficulty from real-time cognitive-load estimates.

papers.title: How Light Shapes Memory: Beta Synchrony in the Temporal-Parietal Cortex Predicts Cognitive Ergonomics for BCI Applications
papers.reason: It analyzes active recall and sequence working-memory tasks using beta-phase synchrony and cross-regional EEG connectivity.

papers.title: Setting up for failure: automatic discovery of the neural mechanisms of cognitive errors
papers.reason: Excluded because the neural validation described in the study uses macaque rather than human participant data.

papers.title: A Quantitative Framework for Assessing Sleep Quality from EEG Time Series in Complex Dynamic Systems
papers.reason: It includes a working-memory task and analyzes EEG phase-amplitude coupling, frequency-specific power, and delta connectivity as sleep-quality markers.

papers.title: Quantifying the Effects of Word Length, Frequency, and Predictability on Dyslexia
papers.reason: It centrally analyzes human eye-tracking measures during naturalistic reading to characterize dyslexic reading costs, without an interactive decision task or neural modality.

papers.title: Working Memory Functional Connectivity Analysis for Dementia Classification using EEG
papers.reason: It combines active working-memory stages with phase-lag and entropy-based EEG functional-connectivity analysis to distinguish healthy, MCI, and Alzheimer groups.

papers.title: Bayesian Distributional Models of Executive Functioning
papers.reason: Excluded because the evaluation uses controlled simulations and synthetically sampled sessions rather than human participants.

papers.title: Bibliometric-enhanced Systematic Literature Review of EEG in Education: Learning Concepts, Computational Methods, and Research Opportunities
papers.reason: The review concerns EEG-based assessment of human attention and learning but does not itself implement an interactive task, eye tracking, qualifying EEG analysis, or cognitive process model.

papers.title: EEG-Based Framework for Reflexive and Perceptual Assessment in CLIS: Preliminary Study in Healthy Volunteers
papers.reason: It uses frequency-tagged steady-state auditory and visual EEG responses in healthy humans for cognitive assessment, but the paradigms are passive rather than behaviorally interactive.

papers.title: EEG-Based Cognitive Load Classification During Landmark-Based VR Navigation
papers.reason: It studies sequential human navigation decisions in VR, but the EEG methodology is described only through classification performance without frequency-domain or connectivity details.

papers.title: A Unified Cortical Circuit Model with Divisive Normalization and Self-Excitation for Robust Representation and Memory Maintenance
papers.reason: Excluded because it is a theoretical neural-circuit study without human participants.

papers.title: Dissociating Cognitive Load and Stress Responses Using Single-Channel EEG: Behavioral and Neural Correlates of Anxiety Across Cognitive States
papers.reason: It investigates human cognitive-load biomarkers, but the auditory tasks lack sufficient interactive-decision detail and the EEG analysis is limited to band summaries and machine-learned features.

papers.title: Sleep Brain and Cardiac Activity Predict Cognitive Flexibility and Conceptual Reasoning Using Deep Learning
papers.reason: It predicts executive-function test performance from passive sleep EEG and ECG, using power-band summaries and deep learning rather than qualifying EEG connectivity or cognitive process modelling.

papers.title: Evaluating Cognitive Assessment Tools:A Comparative Analysis of MMSE, RUDAS, SAGE, ADAS and MoCA for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: It reviews conventional human dementia assessments but contains none of the specified behavioral, eye-tracking, EEG, or computational-modelling methods.

papers.title: Early Detection of Cognitive Impairment in Elderly using a Passive FPVS-EEG BCI and Machine Learning -- Extended Version
papers.reason: It targets early cognitive-impairment detection with passive EEG, but has no behavioral interaction and reports CNN inference without qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Immersive Virtual Reality Assessments of Working Memory and Psychomotor Skills: A Comparison between Immersive and Non-Immersive Assessments
papers.reason: It compares interactive sequential working-memory and reaction-time assessments in VR and on a PC using human performance data.

papers.title: Triangulating LLM Progress through Benchmarks, Games, and Cognitive Tests
papers.reason: Excluded because it benchmarks LLM cognitive abilities using games and tests designed for model evaluation.

papers.title: Interactive Virtual Games: Winners for Deep Cognitive Assessment
papers.reason: It uses interactive minigames with continuous behavioral trajectories to assess working memory, inhibition, processing speed, and rule shifting in a clinical human sample.

papers.title: Mazed and Confused: A Dataset of Cybersickness, Working Memory, Mental Load, Physical Load, and Attention During a Real Walking Task in VR
papers.reason: It records eye tracking and continuous behavior while human participants navigate VR mazes and perform attention and working-memory tasks.

papers.title: Pathformer3D: A 3D Scanpath Transformer for 360° Images
papers.reason: It centrally models human fixation sequences from panoramic eye-tracking datasets, although its objective is scanpath prediction rather than cognitive assessment.

papers.title: Transcranial low-level laser stimulation in near infrared-II region for brain safety and protection
papers.reason: It assesses human executive function and records EEG primarily for stimulation safety, without sufficient task or frequency-domain/connectivity methodology to meet the specified elements.
